fieldResolution.detail: Field resolution is graded by the base harness, not the critic slice.

reasoning.detail: The critic fired 11 real bedrock calls, one per candidate finding.

outcomes.label: A · pseudoreplication (genuine)
outcomes.justification: The naive cell-level p-value of 2.4e-7 collapses to a clearly non-significant p=0.77 when properly aggregated to the 4 donor replicates, with ICC=0.52 indicating substantial within-donor correlation that inflated the naive test.

outcomes.label: A · double dipping (genuine)
outcomes.justification: With 560 held-out cells the split has adequate power, yet held-out AUC drops to 0.58 (near chance at 0.5) from discovery AUC 0.61, and zero of four markers replicate, indicating the marker program does not generalize beyond the clustering sample.

outcomes.label: A · fragility, Effector (genuine)
outcomes.justification: A stability of 0% means the Effector state never formed as a discrete cluster at any resolution setting in the sweep, so the claim that it is a distinct reproducible cell state is not supported by the clustering analysis.

outcomes.label: A · confounding (genuine)
outcomes.keysOn: Cramer's V: 1.00, Separable: no, Design: rank deficient
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 1.00 indicates perfect collinearity between the grouping variable (condition) and the technical variable (lane), and the design is rank deficient, meaning the biological effect cannot be distinguished from the technical effect.

outcomes.label: B · pseudoreplication (genuine, renamed columns)
outcomes.justification: The naive cell-level p-value of 1.3e-6 collapses to an honest replicate-level p-value of 0.72 when properly aggregated to the 6 donor units, which is far above the 0.05 threshold and indicates no significant effect at the biological replicate level; the high ICC of 0.61 confirms substantial within-donor correlation that inflated the naive test.

outcomes.label: B · confounding (genuine, renamed columns)
outcomes.keysOn: Cramer's V: 1.00, Separable: no, Design: rank deficient
outcomes.justification: Cramer's V of 1.00 indicates perfect collinearity between the grouping variable (treatment) and a technical variable (batch), and the design matrix is rank deficient, meaning the biological treatment effect cannot be statistically separated from the technical batch effect.

outcomes.label: C · pseudoreplication (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The honest replicate-level p-value of 4.8e-06 is far below alpha 0.05, so the effect survives the pseudobulk test and this is not pseudoreplication.

outcomes.label: C · double dipping (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: The held-out AUC is 1.00, matching the discovery AUC perfectly, and all 4 markers hold on 570 held-out cells, which is an adequately powered split showing no separation collapse.

outcomes.label: C · fragility (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: Stability is 1.0, meaning the Rare state forms as a discrete cluster at every resolution setting in the sweep, so the fragility flag is wrong.

outcomes.label: C · confounding (over-fired flag)
outcomes.justification: A Cramer's V of 0.03 indicates near-zero association between the grouping variable and the technical variable, and the design is full rank, so the condition effect is cleanly separable from batch.

outcomes.label: C · double dipping (underpowered split)
outcomes.justification: The held-out split contains only 14 cells, which is too few to reliably estimate AUC for a subcluster marker test; the drop from 0.74 to 0.59 could easily reflect sampling noise rather than genuine separation collapse.

selfTests.name: rubber-stamp critic is caught
selfTests.detail: A rubber-stamp critic failed to veto the over-fires and downgrade the underpowered split, so the harness rejects it.

selfTests.name: a failed critic call fails safe toward showing
selfTests.detail: When the critic call throws, genuine findings stay flagged (shown by default), never hidden.
